scope: Migrate sworn's Go status.json carriers (open_deferrals, verification.violations) up to the slice-status-v1 object shape so the autonomous loop reads real coach-produced status without dying on the D6 unmarshal, round-trips it loss-free and byte-stably, reconciles need_ids->covers_needs, adds the inconclusive result enum, and (re-spec) defines the open_deferrals canonical shape STRICTLY and ADDITIVELY (why+tracking+acknowledgement+acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at optional; no anyOf) with the data migrated rather than the schema loosened.

AC-10: open_deferrals required-set is the STRICT ADDITIVE shape [why, tracking, acknowledgement, acknowledged_by] with acknowledged_at optional; the anyOf is REMOVED — slice-status-v1.json. Go Deferral carries named acknowledgement + acknowledged_by + acknowledged_at and round-trips unknown keys via Extra — state.go. Canonical deferral round-trips through Write and validates: TestWrite_CanonicalDeferral_RoundTrips. A deferral missing acknowledgement OR acknowledged_by (OR tracking OR why) fails closed against the schema: TestSchema_OpenDeferralStrictAdditive (acknowledged_by-alone and acknowledgement-alone both FAIL).

AC-11 (tracked cutover/upstream, NOT sworn code this slice): the 127 operator-owned coach deferrals carrying acknowledged_by but no acknowledgement must be migrated to add the Rule-2 plain-text acknowledgement before a strict-schema binary schema-validates them — tracked as #40 (cutover migration). The canonical strict-additive shape is pushed UP to baton (Rule 2 + slice-status-v1) — tracked as #38 (re-purposed from the superseded anyOf framing). Sequencing recorded on both issues: do not run the fired LOOP on a strict binary until the migration is applied.
divergence: AC-10 fail-closed locus (carried forward from session 2, #39): live state.Write calls the structural baton.Validate (which does NOT evaluate open_deferrals item required-sets), not the draft-2020-12 baton.ValidateSchema. So 'a deferral missing acknowledgement OR acknowledged_by SHALL fail closed' is enforced at the CANONICAL SCHEMA/contract layer (proven by the schema-level negative test TestSchema_OpenDeferralStrictAdditive via baton.ValidateSchema), not yet at state.Write runtime. The wholesale Validate->ValidateSchema rewire is the named keystone step-1b follow-up, #39 — out of this slice's AC set (AC-11 scopes the additional work as data-migration + baton-upstream, not the validation rewire). This is the single judgement call; the AC-10 sentence structure (the fail-closed clause has subject 'a deferral', not 'state.Write') supports the schema-layer reading.

Legacy string-form READ tolerance RETAINED (not removed despite the replan's 'no back-compat' theme). Rationale: the replan's concrete S01 instruction was the anyOf->strict-additive required-set change and the AC-11 acknowledgement migration; it did not enumerate removing the string->object read upgrade, and no AC requires or forbids it. Removing it would regress sworn's ability to read its OWN previously-written string-form status.json (every existing board) — a regression NOT covered by the AC-11 migration (which adds acknowledgement to object-form deferrals, it does not convert string->object). The tolerance is a one-way READ upgrade; write-back is always object form; it does not weaken the strict canonical schema. Surfaced for verifier/Coach.

defer_slice (mcp/tools_ops.go) writes a deferral with Why + Acknowledgement but no tracking/acknowledged_by — non-canonical under the strict schema, but state.Write (baton.Validate) does not reject it, so it keeps working. Making it canonical needs a tracking value defer_slice does not have; out of this slice's AC set. Folds under #39 (once state.Write validates strictly, defer_slice's output must be made canonical).

Touchpoints subset: validate_blocked.go (len-only on Violations), router.go and route.go (consume the oracle SliceState.Violations which stays []string) needed no change — fewer files than the planned 16, none outside the track (unchanged from session 2).
